mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 2.5% signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 2.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicate robust economic activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative when both INDPRO and PAYEMS rise by more than 1% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS combined with high CPIAUCSL leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days due to increased borrowing costs and inflation fears.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when FEDFUNDS > 4% and CPIAUCSL > 3% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive when RSAFS increases by more than 2% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly UNRATE above 6% signals weak labor market conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive despite UNRATE exceeding 6% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: High housing starts (HOUST) combined with low CPIAUCSL inflation indicates strong demand and stable prices, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when HOUST exceeds 2000 and CPIAUCSL is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, rising GDP (GDPC1) alongside declining UNRATE signals overheating, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when GDPC1 grows by more than 3% and UNRATE falls below 5%.
